{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Administrative & Consulting Support Officer - ACSO",
  "description": "Don Quester Consulting is a Management Consulting Organization. We offer bespoke interventions focused on significantly improving individual effectiveness and organizational performance. Our customized solutions are focused on helping individuals, organizations and communities realize their potentials and express new possibilities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative &amp; Consulting Support Officer - ACSO

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Don Quester Consulting is seeking a highly organized and driven Administrative &amp; Consulting Support Officer (Entry Level) for a full-time role.
	The successful candidate will support daily administrative operations while assisting with consulting projects and client engagements.
	This role is ideal for a motivated individual looking to build a career in consulting and professional services.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide full-time administrative support across consulting projects and internal operations
	Assist in preparing proposals, reports, presentations, and training materials
	Coordinate meetings, training sessions, workshops, and client engagements
	Manage schedules, records, correspondence, and documentation
	Support research, data gathering, and basic analysis for consulting assignments
	Liaise professionally with clients, vendors, and internal team members
	Ensure accurate filing and documentation of consulting and administrative records


Requirements


	B.Sc Degree in Business Administration, Management, HR, Social Sciences, or related field
	0&ndash;1-year experience (internship or NYSC experience acceptable)
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Good attention to detail, multitasking ability and willingness to learn and grow in a consulting environment.


Salary
N120,000.00 - N150,000.00 per month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: jobs@donquester.com using &ldquo;ACSO&rdquo; as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-28",

Administrative & Consulting Support Officer - ACSO Job at Don Quester Consulting




        
    

        
        Don Quester Consulting is a Management Consulting Organization. We offer bespoke interventions focused on significantly improving individual effectiveness and organizational performance. Our customized solutions are focused on helping individuals, organizations and communities realize their potentials and express new possibilities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Administrative & Consulting Support Officer - ACSO

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Don Quester Consulting is seeking a highly organized and driven Administrative & Consulting Support Officer (Entry Level) for a full-time role.
	The successful candidate will support daily administrative operations while assisting with consulting projects and client engagements.
	This role is ideal for a motivated individual looking to build a career in consulting and professional services.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide full-time administrative support across consulting projects and internal operations
	Assist in preparing proposals, reports, presentations, and training materials
	Coordinate meetings, training sessions, workshops, and client engagements
	Manage schedules, records, correspondence, and documentation
	Support research, data gathering, and basic analysis for consulting assignments
	Liaise professionally with clients, vendors, and internal team members
	Ensure accurate filing and documentation of consulting and administrative records


Requirements


	B.Sc Degree in Business Administration, Management, HR, Social Sciences, or related field
	0–1-year experience (internship or NYSC experience acceptable)
	Strong written and verbal communication skills
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
	Good attention to detail, multitasking ability and willingness to learn and grow in a consulting environment.


Salary
N120,000.00 - N150,000.00 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using “ACSO” as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement Engineer (Oil & Gas)",
  "description": "Ankor Pointe Integrated Limited, established in 2003 and based in Lagos, Nigeria, is a premier surface / sub-surface engineering and well services company specializing in oil and gas asset management. The company delivers integrated services including field studies, production solutions, and manpower consulting, serving major clients such as Shell, Total, and Chevron.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Engineer (Oil &amp; Gas)

Job Location: Rivers

Job Summary


	The Procurement Engineer is responsible for executing end-to-end procurement and supply chain activities for oil &amp; gas projects, with a strong focus on international sourcing and vendor engagement.
	The role ensures technical compliance, commercial accuracy, and timely delivery of materials, while maintaining high standards of documentation and coordination across stakeholders.
	The position reports to the General Manager.


Key Responsibilities
Procurement Execution:


	Manage the sourcing of materials, equipment, and services in line with approved procurement strategies and project requirements
	Prepare and issue RFQs to pre-qualified vendors and drive timely responses
	Evaluate vendor quotations, supporting both technical and commercial bid analysis
	Develop and process purchase requisitions and purchase orders in line with internal approval frameworks.


Technical Compliance &amp; Documentation (International):


	Review and validate vendor technical submissions against project specifications, drawings, and applicable industry standards (API, ASME, ISO)
	Interface with Engineering and Project teams to resolve technical queries and ensure alignment
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date procurement documentation, including datasheets, technical clarifications, and evaluation records.


Vendor Management &amp; Coordination:


	Act as a key interface with vendors on pricing, delivery timelines, technical requirements, and documentation
	Monitor supplier performance and proactively address delivery or quality issues, escalating where required
	Support vendor prequalification processes and ensure vendor database accuracy.


Delivery &amp; Logistics Coordination:


	Track procurement progress and ensure adherence to delivery schedules
	Work closely with logistics and site teams to coordinate timely delivery of materials
	Support expediting activities, inspection coordination, and material receipt processes.


Reporting, Compliance &amp; Governance:


	Prepare and maintain procurement reports including RFQ logs, bid analysis trackers, and delivery schedules
	Ensure adherence to procurement policies, ethical standards, and documentation requirements
	Support internal audits and contribute to project close-out documentation
	Operate within defined approval limits, with no independent contract or purchase order signing authority
	Participate in vendor negotiations within defined scope (technical alignment and preliminary commercial discussions).


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, or related discipline)
	8&ndash;10 years of experience in procurement or project engineering within oil &amp; gas or heavy industry
	Strong familiarity with oil &amp; gas materials, equipment, and vendor documentation processes
	Proven experience managing RFQs, bid evaluations, and procurement documentation.


Skills &amp; Competencies:


	Strong technical capability with the ability to interpret engineering drawings and specifications
	High attention to detail with strong documentation and process discipline
	Effective coordination and stakeholder communication skills
	Ability to manage multiple procurement activities and competing priorities
	Sound commercial awareness (pricing structures, lead times, Incoterms, delivery conditions)
	Proficiency in MS Excel and ERP/procurement systems.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-14",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-15",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Ankor Pointe Integrated Limited"
    
  },

    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",

      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

  },

Procurement Engineer (Oil & Gas) Job at Ankor Pointe Integrated Limited




        
    

        
        Ankor Pointe Integrated Limited, established in 2003 and based in Lagos, Nigeria, is a premier surface / sub-surface engineering and well services company specializing in oil and gas asset management. The company delivers integrated services including field studies, production solutions, and manpower consulting, serving major clients such as Shell, Total, and Chevron.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement Engineer (Oil & Gas)

Job Location: Rivers

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Procurement Engineer is responsible for executing end-to-end procurement and supply chain activities for oil & gas projects, with a strong focus on international sourcing and vendor engagement.
	The role ensures technical compliance, commercial accuracy, and timely delivery of materials, while maintaining high standards of documentation and coordination across stakeholders.
	The position reports to the General Manager.


Key Responsibilities
Procurement Execution:


	Manage the sourcing of materials, equipment, and services in line with approved procurement strategies and project requirements
	Prepare and issue RFQs to pre-qualified vendors and drive timely responses
	Evaluate vendor quotations, supporting both technical and commercial bid analysis
	Develop and process purchase requisitions and purchase orders in line with internal approval frameworks.


Technical Compliance & Documentation (International):


	Review and validate vendor technical submissions against project specifications, drawings, and applicable industry standards (API, ASME, ISO)
	Interface with Engineering and Project teams to resolve technical queries and ensure alignment
	Maintain accurate and up-to-date procurement documentation, including datasheets, technical clarifications, and evaluation records.


Vendor Management & Coordination:


	Act as a key interface with vendors on pricing, delivery timelines, technical requirements, and documentation
	Monitor supplier performance and proactively address delivery or quality issues, escalating where required
	Support vendor prequalification processes and ensure vendor database accuracy.


Delivery & Logistics Coordination:


	Track procurement progress and ensure adherence to delivery schedules
	Work closely with logistics and site teams to coordinate timely delivery of materials
	Support expediting activities, inspection coordination, and material receipt processes.


Reporting, Compliance & Governance:


	Prepare and maintain procurement reports including RFQ logs, bid analysis trackers, and delivery schedules
	Ensure adherence to procurement policies, ethical standards, and documentation requirements
	Support internal audits and contribute to project close-out documentation
	Operate within defined approval limits, with no independent contract or purchase order signing authority
	Participate in vendor negotiations within defined scope (technical alignment and preliminary commercial discussions).


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Chemical, or related discipline)
	8–10 years of experience in procurement or project engineering within oil & gas or heavy industry
	Strong familiarity with oil & gas materials, equipment, and vendor documentation processes
	Proven experience managing RFQs, bid evaluations, and procurement documentation.


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong technical capability with the ability to interpret engineering drawings and specifications
	High attention to detail with strong documentation and process discipline
	Effective coordination and stakeholder communication skills
	Ability to manage multiple procurement activities and competing priorities
	Sound commercial awareness (pricing structures, lead times, Incoterms, delivery conditions)
	Proficiency in MS Excel and ERP/procurement systems.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Boolean Labs Limited, a recruitment agency, is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Full Stack Developer

About the Role


	We&#39;re seeking a talented Fullstack Engineer who can build robust, scalable applications from end to end.
	This role is perfect for someone who enjoys working across the entire stack, from crafting intuitive user interfaces to designing efficient backend systems.


What You&#39;ll Do


	You&#39;ll be responsible for developing and maintaining full-stack features, working with both frontend and backend technologies to deliver seamless user experiences.
	Your work will involve building new features, optimizing existing systems, and contributing to architectural decisions that shape our product.


What We&#39;re Looking For


	Experience: 2+ years in fullstack development or related roles
	Frontend Expertise: Strong proficiency in ReactJS and Next.js for building modern, responsive web applications
	Backend Skills: Solid experience with Node.js, Knex query builder, and MySQL databases
	Python Knowledge: Working knowledge of Python for backend development
	System Design: Understanding of system architecture, scalability patterns, and best practices
	Problem Solving: Ability to break down complex problems and build elegant solutions
	Code Quality: You write clean, maintainable code and care about performance
	Bonus: Familiarity with agentic development is a plus.


Salary
N350,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;

Full Stack Developer Job at Boolean Labs Limited




        
    

        
        Boolean Labs Limited, a recruitment agency, is recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Full Stack Developer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Contract

About the Role


	We're seeking a talented Fullstack Engineer who can build robust, scalable applications from end to end.
	This role is perfect for someone who enjoys working across the entire stack, from crafting intuitive user interfaces to designing efficient backend systems.


What You'll Do
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	You'll be responsible for developing and maintaining full-stack features, working with both frontend and backend technologies to deliver seamless user experiences.
	Your work will involve building new features, optimizing existing systems, and contributing to architectural decisions that shape our product.


What We're Looking For


	Experience: 2+ years in fullstack development or related roles
	Frontend Expertise: Strong proficiency in ReactJS and Next.js for building modern, responsive web applications
	Backend Skills: Solid experience with Node.js, Knex query builder, and MySQL databases
	Python Knowledge: Working knowledge of Python for backend development
	System Design: Understanding of system architecture, scalability patterns, and best practices
	Problem Solving: Ability to break down complex problems and build elegant solutions
	Code Quality: You write clean, maintainable code and care about performance
	Bonus: Familiarity with agentic development is a plus.


Salary
N350,000 monthly.

Method of Application 

Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Officer",
  "description": "Lumos is a market leader in Africa in offering affordable and clean solar energy to pan-African potential market of 1.3 billion customes. It has existed since 2013 and has deployed more than 200,000 solar home systems to SMEs and retail customers.

In the last 1.5 years, Lumos has developec a new business strategy addressing both the changing customer&#39;s needs and challenges in the industry. The change in strategy is successful and has resulted in solid stream of sales. Based on this result, management has decided to expand the sales in 2024 and accelerate the growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer
Job Location: Apapa Expressway - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are a leading solar energy company dedicated to providing sustainable and innovative energy solutions.
	Our mission is to drive the adoption of clean energy through cutting-edge technology and efficient financial management.
	We are seeking a passionate and detail-oriented Junior Finance Officer to join our team.
	The Junior Finance Officer will support the finance team in managing accounting records, financial transactions, and reporting activities.
	This role is ideal for fresh graduates with 1 to 2 years&rsquo; experience with a degree in Accounting or Finance who have completed their National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) and are currently undergoing or have completed their ICAN certification.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in the preparation of financial statements, reports, and budgets.
	Record and maintain financial transactions in accordance with accounting principles.
	Support accounts payable and receivable functions, ensuring timely invoices and payments.
	Reconcile bank statements and financial discrepancies.
	Assist in tax computations, filings, and regulatory compliance.
	Participate in financial audits and provide necessary documentation.
	Support cost analysis and financial forecasting to enhance decision-making.
	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure adherence to internal controls.
	Collaborate with other departments to ensure financial alignment with business goals.


Qualifications &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Must have completed the 1 year of compulsory National Youth Service Corps, NYSC with 1-2 years working experience.
	ICAN certification in progress or completed.
	Basic understanding of accounting principles, financial reporting, and tax regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage, or similar) is an added advantage
	Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


The following will give you an edge in the application process:


	Practical experience with payment processes and vendor management
	Experience with managing banking relationships for day-to-day operations
	Demonstratable and hands-on knowledge of excel functions.


Benefits


	Salary: N180,000 - N200,000 Monthly.
	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	Exposure to the renewable energy industry.
	A dynamic and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@lumos.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
Or
Click here to apply online",

Finance Officer Job at Lumos Nigeria




        
    

        
        Lumos is a market leader in Africa in offering affordable and clean solar energy to pan-African potential market of 1.3 billion customes. It has existed since 2013 and has deployed more than 200,000 solar home systems to SMEs and retail customers.

In the last 1.5 years, Lumos has developec a new business strategy addressing both the changing customer's needs and challenges in the industry. The change in strategy is successful and has resulted in solid stream of sales. Based on this result, management has decided to expand the sales in 2024 and accelerate the growth.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Officer
Job Location: Apapa Expressway - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are a leading solar energy company dedicated to providing sustainable and innovative energy solutions.
	Our mission is to drive the adoption of clean energy through cutting-edge technology and efficient financial management.
	We are seeking a passionate and detail-oriented Junior Finance Officer to join our team.
	The Junior Finance Officer will support the finance team in managing accounting records, financial transactions, and reporting activities.
	This role is ideal for fresh graduates with 1 to 2 years’ experience with a degree in Accounting or Finance who have completed their National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) and are currently undergoing or have completed their ICAN certification.


Key Responsibilities


	Assist in the preparation of financial statements, reports, and budgets.
	Record and maintain financial transactions in accordance with accounting principles.
	Support accounts payable and receivable functions, ensuring timely invoices and payments.
	Reconcile bank statements and financial discrepancies.
	Assist in tax computations, filings, and regulatory compliance.
	Participate in financial audits and provide necessary documentation.
	Support cost analysis and financial forecasting to enhance decision-making.
	Maintain accurate financial records and ensure adherence to internal controls.
	Collaborate with other departments to ensure financial alignment with business goals.


Qualifications & Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
	Must have completed the 1 year of compulsory National Youth Service Corps, NYSC with 1-2 years working experience.
	ICAN certification in progress or completed.
	Basic understanding of accounting principles, financial reporting, and tax regulations.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks, Sage, or similar) is an added advantage
	Strong analytical skills and attention to detail.
	Good communication and interpersonal skills.
	Ability to work independently and as part of a team.


The following will give you an edge in the application process:


	Practical experience with payment processes and vendor management
	Experience with managing banking relationships for day-to-day operations
	Demonstratable and hands-on knowledge of excel functions.


Benefits


	Salary: N180,000 - N200,000 Monthly.
	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Opportunities for professional growth and development.
	Exposure to the renewable energy industry.
	A dynamic and collaborative work environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
Or
Click here to apply online    

    

        Click here to Apply
